input.text: I'm Saksham, a software engineer at Anthropic, based in San Francisco. Working on agent infrastructure.
Hi Alice, can you review the deploy plan for Tuesday? Thanks, Bob
Meeting with Carol and Dave on 2026-04-15. Discussed Q2 hiring plan. Carol pushed back on the timeline.
Loved the new launch! The team really nailed the onboarding flow. Big shoutout to Eve for the design work.
The deployment failed again. Frank's PR broke the staging environment for the third time this week.
Reviewed the codebase architecture today. Key insight: the plugin SDK boundary needs tighter enforcement.
Does anyone know how the cache_control parameter works in the Anthropic SDK?
Released v2.1 on 2026-03-22T14:30:00Z. Hotfix for the auth bug discussed last week.
Excited to share that Grace will be joining as our new VP of Engineering starting May 1st!
This document describes the LLMCallEvent observability sink. It covers the JSONL format, rotation policy, and the per-provider emission contracts.
Today I finished the eval harness work. Tomorrow I'll start on the observability sink wiring.
Sync with Henry, Iris, and Jack about the Q3 roadmap. We aligned on the security workstream priorities.
Decision: we'll deprecate the old auth flow by end of Q2. Karen will own the migration.
Anthropic announced Claude Opus 4.7 today, featuring improved reasoning capabilities and a 1M-token context window.
OK
🎉 Big day — shipped v3.0! Thanks to everyone on the team 🚀

After three months of design discussions involving Lena, Marco, and the platform team, we finalized the new API gateway architecture. The key decision was to adopt edge-side caching with a 60-second TTL, which Marco initially opposed but came around after seeing the latency benchmarks. Implementation starts next sprint.
Standup notes 2026-04-29: Nina demoed the new TUI. Action: Olivia to write tests by Friday.
The CI pipeline is broken AGAIN. Pete needs to fix the flaky voice tests this sprint or we'll keep losing time.
Process started at 14:32:01, completed at 14:32:47. Exit code 0.
The launch went well overall but the analytics dashboard had a bug. Quentin handled the hotfix gracefully under pressure.
Today I learned why prompt caching matters so much for cost. A 30-minute conversation went from $0.40 to $0.05 just by enabling cache_control.
ned to chk if we shipd the bug fxi for the auth flwo
Forwarded message from Rachel: 'The new dashboard is live! Please test by EOD.' — sent 2026-04-30
Approved.
Did Sam ship the migration script yet? Last update was Tuesday.
Another two-hour meeting that could've been a Slack thread. Tara dominated as usual.
See the design doc at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-foo-design.md for full context. Uma reviewed it last week.
Brainstorm output: 5 candidate names — 'Lumina', 'Helix', 'Beacon', 'Pulse', 'Crest'. Vincent and Wendy preferred Lumina.
